title: rubus candicans subsp. adenophorus
intro: Rubus candicans subsp. adenophorus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of bramble in the rose family that is native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus candicans subsp. adenophorus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an anti-diarrheal agent. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or decoction, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
